stories.thread: B.B. King's Recent Hospitalization and Recovery
stories.story: Blues legend B.B. King was recently hospitalized for dehydration, which was caused by his Type II diabetes. His daughter, Claudette King, told the Los Angeles Times that he "is much better." King released a statement Tuesday saying, "I'm feeling much better and am leaving the hospital today." Later that day, Claudette King's publicist, Angela Moore, confirmed he was back home resting and enjoying time with his grandchildren. It is not known where King was hospitalized or if more information on his condition was immediately available.
key_facts: Dehydration caused by Type II diabetes
Claudette King stated he is 'much better'
Angela Moore confirmed he was home resting with grandchildren
explicit_unknowns: More information on King's condition immediately available

stories.thread: B.B. King's Name Origin and Career Milestones
stories.story: B.B. King's stage name, B.B., is an abbreviation for Blues Boy. This name originated from his time as a Memphis disc jockey, where he was known as the Beale Street Blues Boy. King was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987. He has received 30 Grammy nominations throughout his career.
key_facts: B.B. is short for Blues Boy
Name used as Memphis disc jockey, Beale Street Blues Boy
Inducted into Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987

stories.thread: B.B. King's Lucille Guitars and Hit Songs
stories.story: B.B. King, 89, famously named each of his various Gibson guitars 'Lucille.' In the 1980s, Gibson officially designated the model King used most as a custom-made signature guitar, exclusively manufactured for him and named Lucille. Among his notable hits are 'The Thrill Is Gone,' which earned him his first Grammy in 1970, and 'When Love Comes to Town,' a collaboration with U2. Another hit is 'There Must be a Better World Somewhere.'
key_facts: Gibson created custom-made signature Lucille model in 1980s
Hits include 'The Thrill Is Gone' (first Grammy 1970)
Hits include 'There Must be a Better World Somewhere'
Hits include 'When Love Comes to Town' (collaboration with U2)

stories.story: Last year, B.B. King suffered from dehydration and exhaustion after a show in Chicago. This health issue forced him to cancel the remainder of his tour. The exact date of this incident within 'last year' is not specified.
key_facts: Last year, B.B. King suffered dehydration and exhaustion
Incident occurred after a show in Chicago
explicit_unknowns: Exact date of the incident within 'last year'
